ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Vietnamese pho with Angus beef rib; closest parent 'beef noodle soup food product' used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific pho entry; 'Beef soup' (A0FKP) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for Vietnamese pho with Angus beef rib as a prepared dish.

main_ingredients: fresh herbs (bean sprouts, Thai basil, coriander)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant bowl of USDA Angus Beef Rib Pho weighing approximately 600 g, comprising broth, rice noodles, beef rib slices, and fresh herb garnishes.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, short ribs, cooked, braised (USDA SR Legacy 13352) and Rice noodles, cooked (USDA SR Legacy 20113) combined with broth dilution estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Pho is a composite dish. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for braised beef short ribs (FDC 13352), cooked rice noodles (FDC 20113), and a beef bone broth component, weighted by typical pho composition: approximately 25% beef rib meat, 35% rice noodles, 40% broth. Sodium is elevated due to fish sauce and seasoned broth. Values represent a plausible mid-range estimate for a restaurant-prepared bowl.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — rice noodles and mixed beef soup analogue
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese pho with Angus beef rib. GI estimated at 40 based on the primary carbohydrate source being cooked rice noodles (GI ~40–61 per University of Sydney GI Database; fresh rice noodles lower end ~40) combined with the moderating effect of protein-rich beef broth and fat content, which slow gastric emptying. Glycemic load per 600 g serving calculated as: (40 × 58.8 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 23.5. Available carbohydrate for 600 g serving = 9.8 g/100 g × 600 g = 58.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from seasoned broth and fish sauce
hypertension.guidance: A single 600 g bowl provides approximately 2520 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium broth, limit broth consumption, and monitor total daily sodium intake carefully.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from rice noodles offset by protein and fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated glycemic load of 23.5 per bowl is moderate; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a smaller portion of noodles, increase the proportion of beef and broth, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef rib and elevated sodium from broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Beef rib is a higher-fat cut contributing saturated fat (~9 g per bowl); combined with high sodium content, individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion frequency, choose leaner beef cuts when available, and consume only a portion of the broth.

llm_training.natural_language_description: USDA Angus Beef Rib Pho (Vietnamese: Phở Sườn Bò Angus USDA; Chinese: 安格斯牛肋骨河粉) is a premium Vietnamese beef noodle soup served in Singapore, featuring slow-braised USDA-certified Angus beef short ribs in a deeply aromatic bone broth. The broth is prepared by simmering beef bones for many hours with whole spices including star anise, cinnamon, cloves, and charred ginger and onion, producing a rich, complex, slightly sweet and savoury base. Flat rice noodles (bánh phở) are served in the broth alongside tender beef rib meat that falls off the bone. The dish is garnished with fresh bean sprouts, Thai basil, coriander, lime wedges, and sliced chilli, allowing diners to customise flavour and heat. Nutritionally, a 600 g bowl provides approximately 588 kcal, 43 g protein, 23 g fat, and 59 g carbohydrate. The dish is high in sodium (approximately 2520 mg per bowl) due to the seasoned broth and fish sauce, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 40 (low), moderated by protein and fat content, though the glycemic load per bowl is moderate at approximately 23.5. It is a good source of iron, zinc, vitamin B12, and phosphorus from the beef rib component. This dish is popular in Vietnamese restaurants in Singapore and represents a premium restaurant-grade interpretation of a classic Vietnamese street food staple.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a bowl of pho?
What is the glycemic index of pho?
What noodles are used in pho?
What spices are in pho broth?

ingredient_synonyms.USDA Angus: USDA-certified Angus beef, premium Angus beef

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for restaurant-prepared USDA Angus Beef Rib Pho exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for braised beef short ribs and cooked rice noodles, weighted by typical pho composition. GI is an analogous estimate based on rice noodle GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved databases and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data (braised beef short ribs FDC 13352, cooked rice noodles FDC 20113, beef broth) weighted at approximately 25% beef, 35% noodles, 40% broth by mass. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. Sodium value reflects a restaurant-prepared bowl with fish sauce and seasoned broth; actual sodium may vary significantly by restaurant. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate based on fresh rice noodle GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for pho exists in approved sources. Halal status is unconfirmed as USDA Angus beef sourcing and slaughter method are not specified by the restaurant. The dish is served at Viet'Spice - Hương vị Việt - Circular Road, Singapore, at SGD 31.90.
